title: .se

infobox.value: [The Internet Foundation in Sweden](/wiki/The_Internet_Foundation_in_Sweden "The Internet Foundation in Sweden")
Very popular in Sweden. Also used for [domain hacks](/wiki/Domain_hacks "Domain hacks").

["Swedes Abandoning .SE Country Code"](https://web.archive.org/web/20081214052900/http://www.internetnews.com/bus-news/article.php/6_39901). InternetNews.com. 1998-12-14. Archived from [the original](http://www.internetnews.com/bus-news/article.php/6_39901) on 14 December 2008. Retrieved 2009-02-19.

["Sweden's Internet broken by DNS mistake"](https://web.archive.org/web/20091015020048/http://royal.pingdom.com/2009/10/13/sweden%E2%80%99s-internet-broken-by-dns-mistake/). Pingdom. 13 October 2009. Archived from [the original](http://royal.pingdom.com/2009/10/13/sweden%E2%80%99s-internet-broken-by-dns-mistake/) on 15 October 2009. Retrieved 2009-10-13.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Internet country code top-level domain for Sweden

.se

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Introduced | 4 September 1986 |
| [TLD](/wiki/Top-level_domain "Top-level domain") type | [Country code top-level domain](/wiki/Country_code_top-level_domain "Country code top-level domain") |
| Status | Active |
| Sponsor | [The Internet Foundation in Sweden](/wiki/The_Internet_Foundation_in_Sweden "The Internet Foundation in Sweden") |
| Intended use | Entities connected with [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") |
| Actual use | Very popular in Sweden. Also used for [domain hacks](/wiki/Domain_hacks "Domain hacks"). |
| Registered domains | 1,466,636 (31 March 2024)[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Documents | [Terms and regulations for registration](https://internetstiftelsen.se/en/domains/how-to-register-a-domain-name/terms-and-conditions-for-se-and-nu-domains/) |
| Dispute policies | [Alternative dispute resolution (ADR)](https://internetstiftelsen.se/en/dispute-resolution/dispute-resolution-for-se/) |
| [DNSSEC](/wiki/DNSSEC "DNSSEC") | yes |
| Registry website | [internetstiftelsen.se](https://internetstiftelsen.se/) |

**.se**, formerly branded as **.SE**, is the [country code top-level domain](/wiki/Country_code_top-level_domain "Country code top-level domain") (ccTLD) for [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden"). It is operated by [The Swedish Internet Foundation](/wiki/The_Swedish_Internet_Foundation "The Swedish Internet Foundation") (*Internetstiftelsen*), but domains must be registered through one of the approved registrars. The Internet Foundation in Sweden is managed on the basis of its charter of foundation and its statutes. The Foundation is managed by a board of directors, whose decisions are executed by the executive management.

## Pre 2003 system

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=.se&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Pre 2003 system")]

Prior to April 2003, the rules governing domains under the .se top domain were highly restrictive. Only companies, associations and authorities registered nationwide were allowed to register a domain, and it had to be very similar to the registered name. Individual products were not eligible for separate second-level domain names, even if they were [registered trademarks](/wiki/Registered_trademark "Registered trademark"). (Trademarks could register under tm.se but that was not considered satisfactory. Several companies formed daughter companies named after products to circumvent this rule and protect the trademark.) Individuals could register one (and only one) domain with the suffix .pp.se (*pp* is an abbreviation for "private person"), and companies and organizations registered in just a single [county](/wiki/Counties_of_Sweden "Counties of Sweden") were eligible for domains with a <county letter>.se suffix.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Non-profit organisation names are registered on county level in Sweden. Sports clubs do not need to register their name, but that was needed in order to register a "county.se" name, which made it complicated. Many who could not register directly under .se instead registered under [.com](/wiki/.com ".com") or [.nu](/wiki/.nu ".nu"). These rules were introduced in 1996. Before that there were unofficial rules that were even more restrictive, where private people and sports clubs could not register a domain.

The former Second Level Domains were:[[3]](#cite_note-3)

| Domain | Use |
| --- | --- |
| a.se | [Stockholm](/wiki/Stockholm "Stockholm") City |
| b.se | [Stockholm County](/wiki/Stockholm_County "Stockholm County") |
| ac.se | [Västerbotten County](/wiki/V%C3%A4sterbotten_County "Västerbotten County") |
| bd.se | [Norrbotten County](/wiki/Norrbotten_County "Norrbotten County") |
| c.se | [Uppsala County](/wiki/Uppsala_County "Uppsala County") |
| d.se | [Södermanland County](/wiki/S%C3%B6dermanland_County "Södermanland County") |
| e.se | [Östergötland County](/wiki/%C3%96sterg%C3%B6tland_County "Östergötland County") |
| f.se | [Jönköping County](/wiki/J%C3%B6nk%C3%B6ping_County "Jönköping County") |
| g.se | [Kronoberg County](/wiki/Kronoberg_County "Kronoberg County") |
| h.se | [Kalmar County](/wiki/Kalmar_County "Kalmar County") |
| i.se | [Gotland County](/wiki/Gotland_County "Gotland County") |
| k.se | [Blekinge County](/wiki/Blekinge_County "Blekinge County") |
| l.se | [Kristianstad County](/wiki/Kristianstad_County "Kristianstad County") |
| m.se | [Skåne County](/wiki/Sk%C3%A5ne_County "Skåne County") |
| n.se | [Halland County](/wiki/Halland_County "Halland County") |
| o.se | [Västra Götaland County](/wiki/V%C3%A4stra_G%C3%B6taland_County "Västra Götaland County") |
| p.se | [Älvsborg County](/wiki/%C3%84lvsborg_County "Älvsborg County") |
| r.se | [Skaraborg County](/wiki/Skaraborg_County "Skaraborg County") |
| s.se | [Värmland County](/wiki/V%C3%A4rmland_County "Värmland County") |
| t.se | [Örebro County](/wiki/%C3%96rebro_County "Örebro County") |
| u.se | [Västmanland County](/wiki/V%C3%A4stmanland_County "Västmanland County") |
| w.se | [Dalarna County](/wiki/Dalarna_County "Dalarna County") |
| x.se | [Gävleborg County](/wiki/G%C3%A4vleborg_County "Gävleborg County") |
| y.se | [Västernorrland County](/wiki/V%C3%A4sternorrland_County "Västernorrland County") |
| z.se | [Jämtland County](/wiki/J%C3%A4mtland_County "Jämtland County") |
| org.se | Non-profit organizations |
| pp.se | Private persons |
| tm.se | Trademarks |
| parti.se | Political parties |
| press.se | Periodicals |

Only a few of these second level domains are still (2021) used as in active web addresses.

Since å, ä, ö were not available for technical reasons, organisations could register the name with a and o instead if available, sometimes causing trouble. The [Habo](/wiki/Habo_Municipality "Habo Municipality") and [Håbo](/wiki/H%C3%A5bo_Municipality "Håbo Municipality") municipalities had a legal battle about the name habo.se which Håbo won since they registered first. After many years, in 2011, they agreed to make [habo.se](http://www.habo.se/) link to both municipalities' websites. From 2003 Sweden allowed registering å, ä, ö in web addresses.

## Post 2003 system

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=.se&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Post 2003 system")]

With the new rules, any entity or person may register any number of domains, subject to few restrictions. Individuals may register whatever .se domain, as long as it is available, not in [.SE's Blocked or Reserved list](http://www.iis.se/statistics/barred_domains_list.txt). At the same time, the rules for domain name allocation were changed to the principle of first-come, first-served, and simpler rules for [dispute resolution](https://www.iis.se/en/domaner/atf) were created.

As of October 2003, .SE started accepting registrations of [internationalized domain names](/wiki/Internationalized_domain_names "Internationalized domain names"), containing the letters *å*, *ä*, *ö*, *ü* and *é*. On 6 September 2007, a total of 250 characters became available, supporting names in all of the legally recognized [minority languages of Sweden](/wiki/Minority_languages_of_Sweden "Minority languages of Sweden"): Finnish, Meänkieli (Tornedalsfinska), Sami, Romani and Yiddish.

Domain names with å, ä, ö have not seen much use, partly since browsers on the user's side must have special support. As of 2013, organisations having å, ä, ö in their name (like [Skåne](/wiki/Sk%C3%A5ne "Skåne")) mainly use domains without these letters (e.g. [skane.se](http://www.skane.se)), and redirect from their proper name (e.g. [skåne.se](http://www.skåne.se)). Many organisations have however not registered their proper name with å, ä or ö.

There are some second-level TLDs still in use under .se, for example [.domstol.se](/w/index.php?title=.domstol.se&action=edit&redlink=1 ".domstol.se (page does not exist)") reserved for Swedish courts. These might not be recognized by the NIC as second-level TLDs, though in practice they are.

.se is the first TLD to offer [DNSSEC](/wiki/DNSSEC "DNSSEC") services. It does so using [OpenDNSSEC](/wiki/OpenDNSSEC "OpenDNSSEC").

The entire .se domain was unavailable for 58 minutes on 12 October 2009, when an error during routine maintenance by .SE corrupted all names in the [domain name registry](/wiki/Domain_name_registry "Domain name registry").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Known hacks

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=.se&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Known hacks")]

Many Swedish [domains](/wiki/Domain_name "Domain name") were reserved for [English](/wiki/English_language "English language") words that end with "se". As of today, there are practically no such [domain names](/wiki/Domain_name "Domain name") left available on the domain prime market as the result of [domain name speculation](/wiki/Domain_name_speculation "Domain name speculation"). Most of them can be bought on the domain secondary market. Only a few domains were developed.
